prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Ahoy there, matey! Ye be askin' why the sky be blue, ye say? Well, listen here while I spill the tale.

'Tis all about those small specks afloat—what we call ‘light waves’—trapped in the vast sea of the sky. Now, the sun, in all its glory, sends forth beams of all colors, like a shimmerin' rainbow. These beams travel across the horizon to meet our fine blue ocean of air.

You see, this air be chock-full of tiny bits, molecules we're callin' 'em, that be jostlin’ an' buzzin' about just like the scurvy crew in me belly. When the light from our grand old sun voyages through this bustling crowd, it gets scatterin' ‘round like a treasure map in a storm.

But me hearties, not all colors scatter the same—some be bold and some be timid. Blue, that be the scrawniest, tiniest of the colors, scurrrrs through the air quicker than Jack on rum grog, scatterin’ all about our eye-level horizon.

And there ye have it! The blue of the sky be the light that’s scatter’d the most in all directions, a reminder of the endless sea and the freedom it holds. Savvy? Now let’s set course and keep an eye on that horizon!
